title: Mindfulness-based stress reduction for stress management in healthy people: a review and meta-analysis
journal: Journal of alternative and complementary medicine (New York, N.Y.)
headline: Mindfulness-based stress reduction for stress management in healthy people: a review and meta-analysis
summary: A meta-analysis / systematic review of Mindfulness-Based Stress Reduction (MBSR) examined focus & attention. A direct comparison study between MBSR and standard relaxation training found that both treatments were equally able to reduce stress. However, important limitations of the included studies as well as the paucity of evidence about possible specific effects of MBSR in comparison to other nonspecific treatments underline the necessity of further research.
keyFindings: The aim of the present review and meta-analysis is to better investigate current evidence about the efficacy of MBSR in healthy subjects, with a particular focus on its benefits for stress reduction.
A direct comparison study between MBSR and standard relaxation training found that both treatments were equally able to reduce stress.

However, important limitations of the included studies as well as the paucity of evidence about possible specific effects of MBSR in comparison to other nonspecific treatments underline the necessity of further research.
limitations: However, important limitations of the included studies as well as the paucity of evidence about possible specific effects of MBSR in comparison to other nonspecific treatments underline the necessity of further research.
Pooled estimates combine heterogeneous studies; effect sizes vary by practice, population, and control condition.
The practice studied (Mindfulness-Based Stress Reduction (MBSR)) is not the Miracle of Mind practice; findings do not transfer directly to MoM.
simplifiedTitle: Mindfulness-based stress reduction for stress management in healthy people: a review and meta-analysis
journalName: Journal of alternative and complementary medicine (New York, N.Y.)
aiSnippet: Meta-analysis / Systematic review, Journal of alternative and complementary medicine (New York, N.Y.) (2009). The aim of the present review and meta-analysis is to better investigate current evidence about the efficacy of MBSR in healthy subjects, with a particular focus on its benefits for stress reduction. A direct comparison study between MBSR and standard relaxation training found that both treatments were equally able to reduce stress. However, important limitations of the included studies as well as the paucity of evidence about possible specific effects of MBSR in comparison to other nonspecific treatments underline the necessity of further research.
whyItMatters: MoM shares directed attention (the focused-attention component) with the practice studied here. Because the benefit is linked to that mechanism rather than to any one method, it's reasonable to expect it may extend to MoM as well — though MoM hasn't been studied directly for this outcome.
